task.title: Returning a z/OS project to online state
task.shortdesc: If you take a project offline and are ready to connect it to a remote system again, you can return it to online state.

keywords.indexterm: z/OS projectReturning to online stateonline state

taskbody.context: The work offline and work online operations for a remote resource are deprecated.When a project is offline, the resources on the remote system are still accessible to others. You must merge your changes with any changes other users made to resources.

steps.stepsection: To connect a z/OS project back to the remote system and work online, follow these steps:

step.cmd: In the view, right-click the project to be moved online and click Work Online.
step.stepresult: If any of the associated systems are not connected, you are prompted to connect before the project is moved to online state. attempts to resynchronize your local resources with the remote system. A progress indicator is displayed while the product collects out-of-synch resources. If the offline project is large, resynchronization can take a few minutes. After resynchronization, the Work Online window opens showing the resources that changed since the project was taken offline. If you renamed an offline resource, this change shows up as two separate update operations: a deletion of the old resource and an addition of the newly renamed resource.

step.cmd: You can make updates as follows:

substep.cmd: To view differences between a local and remote resource, select the resource of interest in the Changes pane.
substep.stepresult:
A Text Compare pane opens showing the differences between the local and remote resources. You can handle conflicts as follows:
If a file is modified both locally and on the remote system, it is marked with a red diamond. You can copy changes from the remote system to your local workspace by clicking the right arrow between the two panes.
After you fix the conflicts, you can save the local file by right-clicking and selecting Save on the menu.
To indicate that all conflicts are resolved, select the resource in the Changes pane and click Mark as Merged.

substep.cmd: To download a resource that changed on the remote system, select it and click Download.

To upload a resource that changed locally, select it and click Upload. If you choose to overwrite a remote resource when you take a project online, any changes that others made to the resource on the remote system are lost.
To upload the entire subproject, select the subproject name in the Changes pane and click Upload.

step.cmd: After you upload or download all the resources that you want, click Work Online.
